name: 11 What is WHITE BALANCE in photography: A Beginners Guide

step.name: Prep: shoot RAW (max latitude) and pick a neutral picture profile.
step.text: Prep: shoot RAW (max latitude) and pick a neutral picture profile. If colour-critical, keep a grey card handy.

step.name: Choose your method (from quick → precise): Auto WB (AWB): fine for changing d...
step.text: Choose your method (from quick → precise): Auto WB (AWB): fine for changing daylight; can drift in mixed/coloured light.

step.name: Presets: fast and consistent.
step.text: Presets: fast and consistent. Daylight: 5200–5600 K

step.name: Kelvin (K): enable Temp/K on the camera, dial until neutrals look neutral in ...
step.text: Kelvin (K): enable Temp/K on the camera, dial until neutrals look neutral in live view; then nudge Tint (G↔M) if needed. Good starting points are above.

step.name: Custom WB (grey card / ExpoDisc): Place the card in your key light (same ligh...
step.text: Custom WB (grey card / ExpoDisc): Place the card in your key light (same light as your subject).

step.name: Fill the frame with the card and capture a reference.
step.text: Fill the frame with the card and capture a reference.

step.name: Run Custom WB in the menu and assign it to a slot.
step.text: Run Custom WB in the menu and assign it to a slot.

step.name: Use that WB for all shots under the same lighting.
step.text: Use that WB for all shots under the same lighting.
